lines.gurmukhi: ਕਹੈ ਨਾਨਕੁ ਜੀਵਾਲੇ ਜੀਆ ਜਹ ਭਾਵੈ ਤਹ ਰਾਖੁ ਤੁਹੀ ॥੫॥੧੯॥
lines.translit: kahai naanak jeevaale jeea jeh bhaavai teh raakh tuhee |5|19|
lines.english: Says Nanak, He grants life to the living beings; O Lord, please keep me according to Your Will. ||5||19||
Aasaa, First Mehl:
I seek the Name of the Lord and His Praise as my cleansing bath.
Let the body be the Brahmin, and let the mind be the loin-cloth;
let spiritual wisdom be the sacred thread, and meditation the ceremonial ring.
O Pandit, O religious scholar, contemplate God in such a way

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਮੇ ਸੁਚਿ ਨਾਮੋ ਪੜਉ ਨਾਮੇ ਚਜੁ ਆਚਾਰੁ ॥੧॥ ਰਹਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: naame such naamo prrau naame chaj aachaar |1| rahaau |
lines.english: that His Name may sanctify you, that His Name may be your study, and His Name your wisdom and way of life. ||1||Pause||
By Guru's Grace, I am absorbed into God. ||1||
So make the remembrance of the Naam, the Name of the Lord, your loin-cloth and the ceremonial mark on your forehead.

lines.gurmukhi: ਬਾਹਰਿ ਜਨੇਊ ਜਿਚਰੁ ਜੋਤਿ ਹੈ ਨਾਲਿ ॥
lines.translit: baahar janeaoo jichar jot hai naal |
lines.english: The outer sacred thread is worthwhile only as long as the Divine Light is within.
Here and hereafter, the Name alone shall stand by you.

lines.gurmukhi: ਵਿਣੁ ਨਾਵੈ ਹੋਰਿ ਕਰਮ ਨ ਭਾਲਿ ॥੨॥
lines.translit: vin naavai hor karam na bhaal |2|
lines.english: Do not seek any other actions, except the Name. ||2||
Worship the Lord in loving adoration, and burn your desire for Maya.
Behold only the One Lord, and do not seek out any other.
With the diet of His Love, doubt and fear depart.
Become aware of reality, in the Sky of the Tenth Gate;
read aloud the Lord's Word, and contemplate it. ||3||
Let the knowledge of the One God be the ceremonial mark on your forehead.
Let the realization that God is within you be your discrimination. ||4||
With the Lord as your night watchman, no thief will dare to break in.
by reciting sacred scriptures, His value cannot be estimated.
Through ritual actions, God cannot be won over;

lines.gurmukhi: ਅਸਟ ਦਸੀ ਚਹੁ ਭੇਦੁ ਨ ਪਾਇਆ ॥
lines.translit: asatt dasee chahu bhed na paaeaa |
lines.english: The eighteen Puraanas and the four Vedas do not know His mystery.
O Nanak, the True Guru has shown me the Lord God. ||5||20||
He alone is the selfless servant, slave and humble devotee,
Aasaa, First Mehl:
who as Gurmukh, becomes the slave of his Lord and Master.

lines.gurmukhi: ਤਿਸੁ ਬਿਨੁ ਦੂਜਾ ਅਵਰੁ ਨ ਕੋਈ ॥੧॥
lines.translit: tis bin doojaa avar na koee |1|
lines.english: Without Him, there is no other at all. ||1||

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਿਨਿ ਸਿਰਿ ਸਾਜੀ ਤਿਨਿ ਫੁਨਿ ਗੋਈ ॥
lines.translit: jin sir saajee tin fun goee |
lines.english: He, who created the Universe, shall ultimately destroy it.
In the True Court, he is found to be true. ||1||Pause||
The true supplication, the true prayer
Through the Word of the Guru's Shabad, the Gurmukh reflects upon the True Name;
and bestows glorious greatness upon them; that which He wills, comes to pass. ||2||
- within the Mansion of His Sublime Presence, the True Lord Master hears and applauds these.
He summons the truthful to His Heavenly Throne
The Power is Yours; You are my only Support.
With the password of truth, his way is not blocked. ||3||
One who obeys the Hukam of the Lord's Command, goes to Him openly.
The Word of the Guru's Shabad is my true password.
The Pandit reads and expounds on the Vedas,
but he does not know the secret of the thing within himself.

lines.gurmukhi: ਗੁਰ ਬਿਨੁ ਸੋਝੀ ਬੂਝ ਨ ਹੋਇ ॥
lines.translit: gur bin sojhee boojh na hoe |
lines.english: Without the Guru, understanding and realization are not obtained;
but still God is True, pervading everywhere. ||4||
What should I say, or speak or describe?
Only You Yourself know, O Lord of total wonder.
Nanak takes the Support of the Door of the One God.

lines.gurmukhi: ਕਾਚੀ ਗਾਗਰਿ ਦੇਹ ਦੁਹੇਲੀ ਉਪਜੈ ਬਿਨਸੈ ਦੁਖੁ ਪਾਈ ॥
lines.translit: kaachee gaagar deh duhelee upajai binasai dukh paaee |
lines.english: The clay pitcher of the body is miserable; it suffers in pain through birth and death.
Aasaa, First Mehl:
There, at the True Door, the Gurmukhs sustain themselves. ||5||21||

lines.gurmukhi: ਤੁਝ ਬਿਨੁ ਅਵਰੁ ਨ ਕੋਈ ਮੇਰੇ ਪਿਆਰੇ ਤੁਝ ਬਿਨੁ ਅਵਰੁ ਨ ਕੋਇ ਹਰੇ ॥
lines.translit: tujh bin avar na koee mere piaare tujh bin avar na koe hare |
lines.english: Without You, there is no other at all, O my Beloved; without you, there is no other at all.

lines.gurmukhi: ਇਹੁ ਜਗੁ ਸਾਗਰੁ ਦੁਤਰੁ ਕਿਉ ਤਰੀਐ ਬਿਨੁ ਹਰਿ ਗੁਰ ਪਾਰਿ ਨ ਪਾਈ ॥੧॥
lines.translit: eihu jag saagar dutar kiau tareeai bin har gur paar na paaee |1|
lines.english: How can this terrifying world-ocean be crossed over? Without the Lord - Guru, it cannot be crossed. ||1||

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਰਬੀ ਰੰਗੀ ਰੂਪੀ ਤੂੰਹੈ ਤਿਸੁ ਬਖਸੇ ਜਿਸੁ ਨਦਰਿ ਕਰੇ ॥੧॥ ਰਹਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: sarabee rangee roopee toonhai tis bakhase jis nadar kare |1| rahaau |
lines.english: You are in all colors and forms; he alone is forgiven, upon whom You bestow Your Glance of Grace. ||1||Pause||

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਾਸੁ ਬੁਰੀ ਘਰਿ ਵਾਸੁ ਨ ਦੇਵੈ ਪਿਰ ਸਿਉ ਮਿਲਣ ਨ ਦੇਇ ਬੁਰੀ ॥
lines.translit: saas buree ghar vaas na devai pir siau milan na dee buree |
lines.english: Maya, my mother-in-law, is evil; she does not let me live in my own home. The vicious one does not let me meet with my Husband Lord.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਖੀ ਸਾਜਨੀ ਕੇ ਹਉ ਚਰਨ ਸਰੇਵਉ ਹਰਿ ਗੁਰ ਕਿਰਪਾ ਤੇ ਨਦਰਿ ਧਰੀ ॥੨॥
lines.translit: sakhee saajanee ke hau charan sarevau har gur kirapaa te nadar dharee |2|
lines.english: I serve at the feet of my companions and friends; the Lord has showered me with His Mercy, through Guru's Grace. ||2||
